stem: A 20-foot ladder leans against a vertical wall, making a 37-degree angle with the ground. Given that sin 37° ≈ 3/5, cos 37° ≈ 4/5, and tan 37° ≈ 3/4, how high up the wall does the ladder reach, in feet?
explanation: In this right triangle, the ladder is the hypotenuse (20 feet) and the height on the wall is the side opposite the 37° angle. Using sin 37° = opposite/hypotenuse: height = 20 × sin 37° = 20 × (3/5) = 60/5 = 12 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used wrong special triangle ratio — applied the 30-60-90 rule (short leg = hypotenuse/2) instead of using the given trig value: 20/2 = 10
distractorRationale.C: Used tan instead of sin — computed hypotenuse × tan 37° instead of hypotenuse × sin 37°: 20 × (3/4) = 15
distractorRationale.D: Used cos instead of sin — found the base distance (adjacent) instead of the height (opposite): 20 × (4/5) = 16
